set: Commander Legends: Battle for Baldur's Gate

trivia_bullets.text: Talisman of Hierarchy was first printed in Modern Horizons (June 2019, MH1 no. 233), completing the enemy-colored half of the ten-card Talisman cycle. The ally-colored five Talismans existed since Mirrodin (2003); Modern Horizons added the five enemy-color pairs for the first time. This copy is from Commander Legends: Battle for Baldur's Gate (June 2022).

All five enemy-colored Talismans in Modern Horizons were painted by Lindsey Look, who crafted physical clay-and-Swarovski-crystal reference models for each piece. The Talisman of Hierarchy original sold for $4,550 in September 2019 — top lot of the five-piece auction — and featured a chess set as its central design motif.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: Modern Horizons Talisman Cycle by Lindsay Look Sold, 2019

trivia_bullets.text: "You'll never get to the top if you don't know who's already there." — Kaya. Kaya Cassir is a planeswalker ghost-assassin who served as nominal Orzhov guildmaster on Ravnica; her tenure was trapped by a Guildpact clause that assigned all outstanding Orzhov debts to her personally.
sources: MTG Wiki: Kaya Cassir, via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: EDHREC rank 135; appears in approximately 532,000 Commander decks (26.4% inclusion rate). One of the most-played mana rocks in the format. Outperforms Orzhov Signet in many mana-base designs because it taps for colorless without requiring a colored mana input.
sources: EDHREC: Talisman of Hierarchy, via WebSearch
